D4 Colorimetry L4 Analyze Reference

Spectrum to Tristimulus

slide a light's spectrum and watch it integrate against the observer into the three numbers X, Y, Z

The eye reduces an entire spectrum to just three numbers by integrating it against the colour-matching functions — the projection from infinite-dimensional light to 3-D colour.

Slide the light's peak wavelength and width. The grey curve is the spectrum, the faint curves the observer; the bars are the three integrals X, Y, Z, and the swatch is the colour they encode.
